summary:
KEY FUNCTIONALITY: OutputFormat describes the output-specification for a Map-Reduce job.
CORE LOGIC: The Map-Reduce framework relies on the OutputFormat of the job to validate the output-specification of the job and provide the RecordWriter implementation to be used to write out the output files of the job.
INPUTS/OUTPUTS: The output files are stored in a FileSystem.
DEPENDENCIES: The OutputFormat class depends on the RecordWriter and OutputCommitter classes.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- commitJob: moves files from work directory to job output directory
- abortJob: deletes the temporary directory, including all of the work directories
- setupTask: no task setup required
- commitTask: moves files from work directory to job output directory
- abortTask: deletes the work directory
- needsTaskCommit: checks if the task wrote any files in the work directory
- isRecoverySupported: indicates that task recovery is supported
- isCommitJobRepeatable: indicates that commitJob is repeatable
- recoverTask: recovers task output from the previous attempt

CORE LOGIC:
- commitJob: uses algorithmVersion to determine whether to directly merge files from taskAttemptPath to output directory or rename the taskAttemptPath to committedTaskPath
- commitTask: uses algorithmVersion to determine whether to directly merge files from taskAttemptPath to output directory or rename the taskAttemptPath to committedTaskPath
- abortTask: deletes the work directory
- needsTaskCommit: checks if the task wrote any files in the work directory
- isRecoverySupported: indicates that task recovery is supported
- isCommitJobRepeatable: indicates that commitJob is repeatable
- recoverTask: recovers task output from the previous attempt

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- commitJob: takes JobContext as input and does not produce any output
- abortJob: takes JobContext as input and does not produce any output
- setupTask: takes TaskAttemptContext as input and does not produce any output
- commitTask: takes TaskAttemptContext as input and does not produce any output
- abortTask: takes TaskAttemptContext as input and does not produce any output
- needsTaskCommit: takes TaskAttemptContext as input and does not produce any output
- isRecoverySupported: takes no input and does not produce any output
- isCommitJobRepeatable: takes JobContext as input and does not produce any output
- recoverTask: takes TaskAttemptContext as input and does not produce any output

DEPENDENCIES:
- org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.output.FileOutputCommitter
- org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.JobContext
- org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.JobStatus
- org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.TaskAttemptContext
- org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.TaskAttemptID
- org.apache.hadoop.fs.FileSystem
- org

summary:
KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

Creates a FileOutputCommitter, always.

CORE LOGIC:

None visible.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

Input: Path outputPath, TaskAttemptContext context
Output: FileOutputCommitter

DEPENDENCIES:

org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.output.FileOutputCommitter
org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.TaskAttemptContext
org.apache.hadoop.fs.Path
java.io.IOException

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- The FileOutputFormat class is a base class for output formats that read from FileSystems.
- It provides methods for setting and getting the output path, compressing the output, and generating unique file names.
- It also provides a method for getting the default work file path and filename for the output format.

CORE LOGIC:
- The FileOutputFormat class provides a template method for getting the record writer, which is responsible for writing the output data.
- It also provides methods for checking the output specifications and getting the output committer.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The FileOutputFormat class takes input from the job context and task attempt context, and provides output in the form of a record writer and output committer.

DEPENDENCIES:
- The FileOutputFormat class depends on the OutputCommitter and RecordWriter classes.
- It also depends on the PathOutputCommitter and PathOutputCommitterFactory classes.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This chunk of code is a FilterOutputFormat class that extends the OutputFormat class. It is a convenience class that wraps OutputFormat.

CORE LOGIC:
The core logic of this chunk is to provide a way to filter the output of an OutputFormat. It does this by implementing the getRecordWriter, checkOutputSpecs, and getOutputCommitter methods, which are the key methods in the OutputFormat class.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The inputs to this chunk are the base OutputFormat and the context in which it is being used. The outputs are the filtered output of the base OutputFormat.

DEPENDENCIES:
The dependencies of this chunk are the OutputFormat class and its parent classes. It also depends on the RecordWriter class, which is used to write the output.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY: LazyOutputFormat is a convenience class that creates output lazily.
It is used in conjunction with org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.output.MultipleOutputs to recreate the
behaviour of org.apache.hadoop.mapred.lib.MultipleTextOutputFormat (etc) of the old Hadoop API.

CORE LOGIC: The core logic of LazyOutputFormat is to create output lazily, i.e., only when it is needed.
This is achieved by using a LazyRecordWriter class that extends FilterRecordWriter and overrides the write()
and close() methods to create the RecordWriter only when it is needed.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS: LazyOutputFormat takes an OutputFormat class as input and creates an instance of it only when
required. It then uses the instance to create a RecordWriter for writing the output.

DEPENDENCIES: LazyOutputFormat depends on the OutputFormat class specified in the configuration. It also
depends on the LazyRecordWriter class for creating the RecordWriter lazily.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

The MapFileOutputFormat class is an OutputFormat that writes MapFile objects.

CORE LOGIC:

The getRecordWriter() method returns a RecordWriter that writes MapFile objects.

The getReaders() method returns an array of MapFile.Reader objects.

The getEntry() method returns an entry from output generated by this class.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The getRecordWriter() method takes a TaskAttemptContext object as input and returns a RecordWriter object.

The getReaders() method takes a Path object and a Configuration object as input and returns an array of MapFile.Reader objects.

The getEntry() method takes an array of MapFile.Reader objects, a Partitioner object, a WritableComparable object, and a Writable object as input and returns a Writable object.

DEPENDENCIES:

The MapFileOutputFormat class depends on the following classes:

FileOutputFormat
SequenceFileOutputFormat
MapFile
FileSystem
Path
FileUtil
PathFilter
WritableComparable
Writable
SequenceFile.CompressionType
CompressionCodec
DefaultCodec
Partitioner
RecordWriter
TaskAttemptContext
Configuration
ReflectionUtils
InterfaceAudience
InterfaceStability

KEY FUNCTIONALITY: Simplifies writing output data to multiple outputs.

CORE LOGIC: Supports counters, by default they are disabled. The counters group is the MultipleOutputs class name. The names of the counters are the same as the output name. These count the number records written to each output name.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS: Data flowing in and out.

DEPENDENCIES: Key imported, parent, or collaborator classes visible in this chunk.

- The MultipleOutputs class is a wrapper for writing to multiple output files in a Hadoop MapReduce job.
- It provides a way to write key-value pairs to different output files based on a given key.
- The class implements the OutputCommitter interface to handle the commit and abort of the output files.

- The MultipleOutputs class uses a Map to store the RecordWriter objects for each output file.
- It provides methods to write key-value pairs to the appropriate RecordWriter based on the key.
- The class also provides methods to get the RecordWriter for a specific output file and to close all the opened outputs.

- The MultipleOutputs class takes a TaskAttemptContext object as a parameter in its constructor.
- It uses the TaskAttemptContext to get the Configuration object and to create RecordWriter objects for the output files.
- The class provides methods to write key-value pairs to the appropriate RecordWriter based on the key.
- It also provides methods to get the RecordWriter for a specific output file and to close all the opened outputs.

- The MultipleOutputs class depends on the following classes:
  - OutputCommitter: to handle the commit and abort of the output files.
  - TaskAttemptContext: to get the Configuration object and to create RecordWriter objects for the output files.
  - RecordWriter: to write key-value pairs to the output files.
  - Configuration: to get the configuration parameters for the job.

summary:
KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- The NamedCommitterFactory class is a factory that creates any named committer identified in the option org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.output.NamedCommitterFactory.NAMED_COMMITTER_CLASS.

CORE LOGIC:
- The createOutputCommitter method creates an instance of the named committer class using reflection.
- The loadCommitterClass method loads the committer class named in the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.output.NamedCommitterFactory.NAMED_COMMITTER_CLASS option.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The createOutputCommitter method takes a Path and a TaskAttemptContext as input and returns a PathOutputCommitter.
- The loadCommitterClass method takes a JobContext as input and returns a Class<? extends PathOutputCommitter>.

DEPENDENCIES:
- The NamedCommitterFactory class depends on the org.apache.hadoop.conf.Configuration, org.apache.hadoop.fs.Path, org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.JobContext, and org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.TaskAttemptContext classes.
- The createOutputCommitter method depends on the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.output.PathOutputCommitter class.
- The loadCommitterClass method depends on the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.output.FileOutputCommitter class.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This class is an implementation of the OutputFormat class, which is used to control the format of the data that is output by the MapReduce framework.

CORE LOGIC:
The core logic of this class is to consume all outputs and put them in /dev/null.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The inputs to this class are the data that is output by the MapReduce framework, and the output is /dev/null.

DEPENDENCIES:
This class depends on the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce package, which provides the MapReduce framework for processing large-scale data.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY: commits files specified in job output directory
CORE LOGIC: removes any committed output for a task
INPUTS/OUTPUTS: data flowing in and out
DEPENDENCIES: key imported, parent, or collaborator classes visible in this chunk

- Interface for an OutputCommitter implementing partial commit of task output
- During preemption

CORE LOGIC:
- None visible in this chunk

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- None visible in this chunk

DEPENDENCIES:

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
The PathOutputCommitterFactory class is a factory for committers implementing the PathOutputCommitter methods, and can be used from FileOutputFormat.

CORE LOGIC:
The algorithm for creating a committer factory is as follows:
1. If an explicit committer factory is named, it is used.
2. The output path is examined. If it is non-null and there is an explicit schema for that filesystem, its factory is instantiated.
3. Otherwise, an instance of FileOutputCommitter is created.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The createOutputCommitter method takes a Path and a TaskAttemptContext as inputs and returns a new committer.

DEPENDENCIES:
The PathOutputCommitterFactory class depends on the following classes:
- Configured: for configuration-related functionality
- PathOutputCommitter: for committer functionality
- FileOutputCommitter: for default committer functionality
- TaskAttemptContext: for task attempt context functionality
- Configuration: for configuration settings
- Path: for path-related functionality
- IOException: for handling input/output errors

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This chunk of code is an implementation of the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.OutputFormat interface that writes keys and values to SequenceFile s in binary (raw) format.

CORE LOGIC:

The core logic of this chunk of code is the implementation of the getRecordWriter method, which returns a RecordWriter that writes BytesWritable keys and BytesWritable values to a SequenceFile. The RecordWriter uses the SequenceFile.Writer's appendRaw method to write the raw bytes of the key and value to the SequenceFile.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The input to this chunk of code is a TaskAttemptContext, which contains information about the task attempt, such as the task ID, the job configuration, and the output directory. The output of this chunk of code is a SequenceFile that contains the binary (raw) representation of the keys and values.

DEPENDENCIES:

This chunk of code depends on the following classes:

org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.output.SequenceFileOutputFormat: This class provides the base implementation for writing to SequenceFile s.
org.apache.hadoop.io.BytesWritable: This class represents a byte array that can be used as a key or value in a SequenceFile.
org.apache.hadoop.io.SequenceFile.Writer: This class is used to write keys and values to a SequenceFile.
org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.RecordWriter: This interface is used to write records to an output format.
org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.TaskAttemptContext: This interface provides information about the task attempt, such as the task ID, the job configuration, and the output directory.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

The SequenceFileOutputFormat class is an OutputFormat that writes SequenceFile objects.

CORE LOGIC:

The getSequenceWriter method creates a SequenceFile.Writer object, which is used to write key-value pairs to a SequenceFile. The method takes a TaskAttemptContext, keyClass, and valueClass as parameters, and returns the created SequenceFile.Writer object.

The getRecordWriter method creates a RecordWriter object that writes key-value pairs to the SequenceFile created by the getSequenceWriter method.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The SequenceFileOutputFormat class takes key-value pairs as input and writes them to a SequenceFile.

DEPENDENCIES:

The SequenceFileOutputFormat class depends on the SequenceFile class from the Apache Hadoop MapReduce library.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
The main purpose of this chunk is to write plain text files.

CORE LOGIC:
The key algorithm or processing pattern visible in this chunk is the LineRecordWriter class, which is used to write key-value pairs to a byte stream, handling Text as a special case.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The data flowing in is the key-value pairs to be written, and the data flowing out is the written text files.

DEPENDENCIES:

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This chunk of code provides user-facing APIs for transferring secrets from the job client to the tasks.

CORE LOGIC:
The code includes methods for obtaining delegation tokens from namenodes corresponding to the paths passed, as well as methods for removing jobtoken referrals that don't make sense in the context of the task execution.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The code takes in a Credentials object, an array of paths, and a Configuration object as inputs. It also outputs a Credentials object with updated tokens.

DEPENDENCIES:
The code depends on the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.security package and the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.security.token package.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY: The main purpose of this chunk is to define an annotation representing that the task can be safely preempted and restarted between invocations of the user-defined function.

CORE LOGIC: The annotation is used to indicate that the task can be checkpointed and restarted, which allows for fault tolerance and efficient processing of large datasets.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS: The annotation has no inputs or outputs.

DEPENDENCIES: The annotation is used to decorate a class, so it does not have any dependencies on other classes or packages.
